title: Aneilema hamiltonianum
intro: Aneilema hamiltonianum, also known as the "Water Lily Tuber", is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the Commelinaceae family. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Aneilema hamiltonianum include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Aneilema hamiltonianum belongs to the family Commelinaceae, commonly known as the spiderwort family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Aneilema. The Commelinaceae family is a large group of flowering plants, comprising around 600 species. These plants are primarily found in tropical regions, with some species extending into subtropical areas. Aneilema hamiltonianum is native to the tropical regions of Asia.
